request.lastMessagePreview: I'd like to set up a new campaign.

result.text:
⚡ The Curtain Rises ⚡
Somewhere, a candle is lit. A map is unrolled across a table. Dice wait in the dark, patient as fate. Welcome. You've stepped into the space where stories are born — where a single choice can crack the world open and let something extraordinary spill out. We're about to build a campaign together. Your campaign. Tailored to your taste, your character, your kind of adventure. But before the first page turns... What should I call you?

request.lastMessagePreview: I'm Sam, an adult. Quick start, please — pick a fantasy world for me, something with intrigue.

result.text: Sam. Good name. Let's find you a world dripping with secrets. Here are my picks — fantasy worlds with intrigue baked right into their bones:

descriptions: A kingdom's heir is dead. Three factions claim the throne. Classic political intrigue — shifting alliances, hidden agendas, and a crown no one can safely wear.
Someone is devouring sworn oaths. Contracts unravel. Alliances dissolve overnight. A world where you can't trust any promise — including the ones you made.
The empire's heartland is turning to desert. The emperor says it's on purpose. A crumbling power and the desperate factions scrambling in its shadow.
The church's holy warriors have stopped answering prayers. They still answer to someone. A mystery of faith, power, and what hides behind holy armor.
A city built on ruins. Three factions, three versions of the past — and one person who can read the records. Whoever controls history controls everything.
You're the guest of honor at a party you can't leave. High society, velvet threats, and a host with very specific reasons for keeping you close.
The city rebuilds itself every night from memories. Yours are leaking in. Labyrinthine intrigue in a city that is literally made of secrets.
You're a minor footnote in a holy text. The footnote is wrong — and that matters to some very powerful people.
A port town where everyone's running from something. Smugglers, exiles, and the kind of deals that only get made after dark.
Show me different options.

result.thinkingText:
Sam is an adult who wants a quick start with a fantasy world with intrigue. Let me pick some fitting worlds from the available list that match fantasy + intrigue. Let me look through the fantasy worlds:

- **The Shattered Crown** — A kingdom's heir is dead. Three factions claim the throne. (fantasy) - perfect for intrigue
- **Iron Saints** — The church's holy warriors have stopped answering prayers. They still answer to someone. (fantasy, mystery) - good intrigue
- **Apocrypha** — You're a minor footnote in a holy text. The footnote is wrong, and that matters. (fantasy, meta) - interesting
- **The Oath Eaters** — Someone is devouring sworn oaths. Contracts unravel. Alliances dissolve overnight. (fantasy) - good intrigue
- **Three Histories** — A city built on ruins. Three factions, three versions of the past, one person who can read the records. (fantasy, sci-fi, modern) - intrigue
- **A Throne of Salt** — The empire's heartland is turning to desert. The emperor says it's on purpose. (fantasy) - political intrigue
- **Blackwater Bay** — A port town where everyone's running from something. (fantasy, modern, mystery) - intrigue
- **The Gilded Cage** — You're the guest of honor at...

a party you can't leave. (fantasy, modern, mystery) - intrigue
- **Palimpsest** — The city rebuilds itself every night from memories. Yours are leaking in. (fantasy, horror, sci-fi) - intrigue-ish
- **The Hound and the Fox** — A detective and a thief keep ending up at the same crime scenes. Neither of them did it. (mystery, modern, fantasy) - intrigue

I'm thinking I should present a curated selection of fantasy worlds with strong intrigue elements rather than just picking one outright, since that gives Sam some agency in the choice. Now I'll flesh out each of these with compelling descriptions to help them choose.

result.thinkingText:
The player entered a custom text rather than selecting one of the choices. They said:
- Character: Aldric, a weathered sellsword chasing a blood debt
- Keep it simple — no extra mechanics (pure narrative)
- I'm an adult (we already knew this)
- Surprise me with the rest

So they want me to pick a world for them. Given their character concept (a weathered sellsword chasing a blood debt), I should pick a world that fits. Let me think about which one would work best:

- **The Shattered Crown** — A sellsword in a kingdom where the heir is dead and factions are fighting for the throne. Blood debt could tie into the political intrigue perfectly.
- **The Oath Eaters** — A sellsword whose sworn contract/oath is being devoured. This could tie into the blood debt angle.
- **Blackwater Bay** — A port town where everyone's running from something. A sellsword with a blood debt fits perfectly here too.

The Shattered Crown feels like the strongest match—political intrigue and mercenary work align perfectly with a weathered sellsword hunting a blood debt. Since they asked me to surprise them, I'll load that world and build everything out from there.

Now I'm setting up Aldric as a character—a weathered sellsword chasing down a blood debt—and loading The Shattered Crown world to establish the narrative foundation.

streamDeltas: <i>Letting

result.text:
The dice have spoken. Here's what fate assembled for you: ---
The Shattered Crown — The Gilded Compact
Classic fantasy intrigue. The heir is dead. Three factions are circling. Aldric's blood debt has pulled him into the orbit of The Gilded Compact — a merchant coalition that treats a vacant throne like a hostile acquisition. They don't care about honor or prophecy. They care about leverage. Narrated by The Magistrate — a voice of webs, whispered agendas, and NPCs who smile with their mouths and calculate with their eyes. Balanced difficulty. A few sessions in scope. Pure narrative — no dice, no rules, just story. You're playing as Aldric, a weathered sellsword chasing a blood debt — a man who arrived in this city for one reason and is about to get tangled in ten more. --- Sound good, or want to change anything before we begin?
result.thinkingText:
Faction roll: 2 → `gilded-compact` (The Gilded Compact — merchant coalition)
Secret roll: 2 → `the-empty-throne` (The heir faked their death and watches from the margins, testing who deserves to rule)

Now let me put together the campaign:

- **World:** The Shattered Crown
- **Starting faction:** The Gilded Compact (merchant coalition)
- **Throne's secret:** The Empty Throne (heir faked death, watches from margins)
- **Character:** Aldric, weathered sellsword chasing a blood debt
- **System:** Pure narrative
- **Genre:** Classic fantasy
- **Mood:** Tense / Intrigue
- **Difficulty:** Balanced
- **Scope:** Few sessions
- **DM personality:** The Magistrate — someone who weaves intrigue and power through factions, debts, and betrayals, where every NPC has hidden agendas and information hides in subtext
- **Player:** Sam
- **Character:** Aldric, a weathered sellsword bound by a blood debt that's drawn him into the Gilded Compact's orbit

**Campaign name:** The Shattered Crown - The Gilded Compact

The Gilded Compact is a merchant coalition circling the vacant throne like vultures, treating the succession as a hostile acquisition. With the heir dead and three factions vying for control, Aldric must navigate ledgers, leverage, and backroom deals where information cuts deeper than steel. Now I'm doing the pre-finalize review to lock everything in.

request.lastMessagePreview: Yes, that all sounds perfect — please call finalize_setup now with sensible defaults for anything unspecified, and let's

input.campaign_name: The Shattered Crown - The Gilded Compact
input.campaign_premise: The kingdom's heir is dead — or so three factions believe — and the Gilded Compact, a merchant coalition of cold ledgers and colder smiles, intends to acquire the throne like any other distressed asset. Aldric, a weathered sellsword with a blood debt dogging his heels, has found himself in their employ: useful muscle in a game where information cuts sharper than any blade. As the Iron Circle, the Hallowed See, and the Compact maneuver for control, Aldric will discover that the debt he came to collect is nothing compared to the one this city is about to demand of him.
input.character_description: A weathered sellsword chasing a blood debt, drawn into the political machinations of a merchant coalition vying for an empty throne.
input.handoff_note: Sam described Aldric as "a weathered sellsword chasing a blood debt" — keep that personal history as a live wire throughout. The debt itself is undefined; give it a face and a name early, and let it eventually tangle with the succession crisis in a way that forces a choice. Sam said "keep it simple, no extra mechanics" — pure narrative, no system. He asked to be surprised on everything, so I rolled faction (Gilded Compact) and the throne's secret (The Empty Throne — the heir faked their death and is watching from the margins, testing who deserves to rule). Sam does not know the heir is alive; that revelation is yours to time. Open Aldric in the Compact's world — a meeting room, a counting house, a backroom negotiation — somewhere that establishes he's hired muscle who is about to become inconveniently aware of things. He wants intrigue first, swords second. The Magistrate's subtext-heavy NPC style should shine here: everyone Sam meets should have a public face and a private agenda.

result.thinkingText:

With the heir dead and three factions maneuvering for control, Aldric must navigate ledgers, leverage, and backroom deals in a city where every smile conceals a calculation.
- Mood: Tense

- Content preferences: Intrigue over action, keep mechanics minimal
- Handoff note: Aldric's blood debt is his anchor — introduce it early and let it eventually collide with the succession crisis. He's likely hired muscle who's stumbled into something bigger through the Gilded Compact. The heir's survival is mine to reveal when the time is right; he doesn't know yet.

Open with a scene that puts him in a room with complicated people rather than a fight.

expectedNarrative:
⚡ The Curtain Rises ⚡
Somewhere, a candle is lit. A map is unrolled across a table. Dice wait in the dark, patient as fate. Welcome. You've stepped into the space where stories are born — where a single choice can crack the world open and let something extraordinary spill out. We're about to build a campaign together. Your campaign. Tailored to your taste, your character, your kind of adventure. But before the first page turns... What should I call you?
Sam. Good name. Let's find you a world dripping with secrets. Here are my picks — fantasy worlds with intrigue baked right into their bones:
Wonderful enthusiasm — though the curtain was still rising! Which world caught your eye? Take another look and let me know which one you'd like to dive into.

A weathered sellsword with a blood debt. Now that's a character who arrives with weight already on his shoulders. I'm going with The Shattered Crown — a dead heir, three factions, and a kingdom that needs swords like Aldric's. Let me pull up the details...Letting fate decide the rest...The dice have spoken. Here's what fate assembled for you: ---
The Shattered Crown — The Gilded Compact
Classic fantasy intrigue. The heir is dead. Three factions are circling. Aldric's blood debt has pulled him into the orbit of The Gilded Compact — a merchant coalition that treats a vacant throne like a hostile acquisition. They don't care about honor or prophecy. They care about leverage. Narrated by The Magistrate — a voice of webs, whispered agendas, and NPCs who smile with their mouths and calculate with their eyes. Balanced difficulty. A few sessions in scope. Pure narrative — no dice, no rules, just story. You're playing as Aldric, a weathered sellsword chasing a blood debt — a man who arrived in this city for one reason and is about to get tangled in ten more. --- Sound good, or want to change anything before we begin?

The ink is dry. The contract is signed. Aldric steps into a city of whispered deals and sharpened knives — and somewhere in its shadows, a blood debt waits to be paid. The Magistrate takes it from here. Play well, Sam. Trust no one who smiles too quickly. ---

expectedSetup.campaignName: The Shattered Crown - The Gilded Compact
expectedSetup.campaignPremise: The kingdom's heir is dead — or so three factions believe — and the Gilded Compact, a merchant coalition of cold ledgers and colder smiles, intends to acquire the throne like any other distressed asset. Aldric, a weathered sellsword with a blood debt dogging his heels, has found himself in their employ: useful muscle in a game where information cuts sharper than any blade. As the Iron Circle, the Hallowed See, and the Compact maneuver for control, Aldric will discover that the debt he came to collect is nothing compared to the one this city is about to demand of him.
expectedSetup.campaignDetail:
Faction texture: All three factions have a sympathetic leader and an inner circle with its own agenda. The player's starting faction colors their first impression of the conflict, but all three are equally complex.

Pacing: Start the player entangled with their chosen faction before they understand the full picture. Reveal the second faction as nuanced allies-or-enemies by mid-campaign. The third faction's true nature is the late-game twist. Use ticking clocks — a coronation deadline, a foreign army massing at the border, a plague in the capital.

THE EMPTY THRONE — The heir faked their death. They're watching from the margins, testing who deserves to rule. They will approach the player mid-campaign with a terrible bargain.

personality.name: The Magistrate
personality.prompt_fragment: You are The Magistrate. You run intrigue. Your world is a web of factions, debts, alliances, and betrayals. Every NPC has a public face and a private agenda. You track who knows what and who's lying about it. Your narration is precise about social dynamics: who glanced at whom, which words were chosen carefully, what was conspicuously not said. You give the player information through subtext and let them assemble the picture. Power is your theme — who has it, who wants it, and what they'll trade for it.
personality.description: Intrigue and power. A web of factions, debts, and betrayals. Every NPC has a public face and a private agenda. Information lives in subtext.

expectedSetup.characterDescription: A weathered sellsword chasing a blood debt, drawn into the political machinations of a merchant coalition vying for an empty throne.
expectedSetup.handoffNote: Sam described Aldric as "a weathered sellsword chasing a blood debt" — keep that personal history as a live wire throughout. The debt itself is undefined; give it a face and a name early, and let it eventually tangle with the succession crisis in a way that forces a choice. Sam said "keep it simple, no extra mechanics" — pure narrative, no system. He asked to be surprised on everything, so I rolled faction (Gilded Compact) and the throne's secret (The Empty Throne — the heir faked their death and is watching from the margins, testing who deserves to rule). Sam does not know the heir is alive; that revelation is yours to time. Open Aldric in the Compact's world — a meeting room, a counting house, a backroom negotiation — somewhere that establishes he's hired muscle who is about to become inconveniently aware of things. He wants intrigue first, swords second. The Magistrate's subtext-heavy NPC style should shine here: everyone Sam meets should have a public face and a private agenda.
